items.summary:
При запуске игры, на моменте проверки обновлений ресурсов пишет: Сеть занята, повторите попытку позже.
Собственно поиграть нельзя, так-как игра даже не запускается.

items.title: [正面] This is the 'free' model of "Chrono Ark" which, pe...
items.summary:
This is the 'free' model of "Chrono Ark" which, personally, I have been finding just as enjoyable. I know that there are a great many western gamers who will find this statement troublesome to swallow but -- this hobby (like most) is entirely a waste of time and money if one believes that games/art/non-GDP productive activity is wasteful.

So, if you want to just spend your money upfront and dump your personal metric of hours per cash-units on what is likely to be a singularly enjoyable, but also singularly developed, endeavor - go with something like ChArk. If you like to engage in the pretense of a continually developed endeavor that, theoretically, will drip feed you similar content so long as you continually engage in the pretense of communal drip feeding of funds in return - then I think this is already a good "living" example of something that plays around with 4D randomized deckbuilding nonsense.

That said - this isn't exactly hiding its overall business model. I think it's doing a pretty fair job about it. It doesn't feel predatory nor does the prices or what's being asked for what's being offered feel criminal. In some ways that is more difficult to navigate. I hope others will view the limits put on it as signals to, like, ya know -- step away and do something else for awhile instead of forcing money into the dopamine tube of the moment. Which, hey, spend 'em if ya got 'em and I certainly made poor choices with my time/health with even ChrnRk in the endeavor to unlock whatever idiocy or finally understand some piece of wild mechanics synergy after just "one more" run that makes such a warning hypocritical. Still, I can see the ways in which this could be problematic for some despite what does feel like overall best efforts on the development team.

Anyway, I do think that the vibe is fun - the designs hint at a openness of thought and the mechanics are solid. I wish it weren't so mud-paced, but then -- the model is about that and visual novels have a certain hitch to them that's inescapable. I think that if you know yourself well enough to not get sucked to far down the money fire pit nor be too disappointed when this inevitable gets bought out by some more predatory company and/so just disappears all together - one could have a fun time.

if you find the doom/gloom elements of a review like this worrisome -- well then, just save enough money for CArk and sink the precious hours of your precious, but ultimately finite, life into that instead since I don't think you'll find much beyond pure, high grade 'novelty' to snort from this vs. one-time purchases [I mean, not that any of us own anything here on Steam, but all y'all fellow degenerates know what I mean by 'purchase' and 'money' and 'own' here and if not then -- well; this might be a good time for one to consider some of those topics and contemplate a return to hard disk formats and closed loop hardware :3]
